title: Dual wavelength source of entanglement for space quantum communication
authors: Valentin Dumas, Alek Lagarrigue, Tess Troisi, Gregory Sauder, Sebastien Tanzilli, Anthony Martin, Olivier Alibart
abstract: We report the demonstration of a bulk, intrinsically phase-stable source of polarization- and time-energy-entangled photon pairs at 810nm and 1550nm, directly coupled into single-mode optical fibers. This highly non-degenerate wavelength combination is well suited for hybrid quantum communication networks, enabling low-loss transmission in optical fibers at 1550nm while maintaining efficient free-space propagation and detection at 810nm. The source is based on spontaneous parametric down-conversion in a periodically poled lithium niobate crystal embedded in a polarization Sagnac interferometer, providing inherent stability and dual-degree-of-freedom entanglement.   We measure a spectral brightness of B = 4800 pair/s/mW/GHz, with fiber coupling efficiencies exceeding 0.48 at both wavelengths. The entanglement quality is characterized by high-visibility two-photon interference, yielding net visibilities of 0.995 in the polarization basis and 0.991 in the energy-time basis. These performances demonstrate a compact and robust entanglement source compatible with hybrid fiber/free-space quantum key distribution architectures, and suitable for future ground-to-satellite quantum communication links.
summary: The researchers developed a dual-wavelength entanglement source that operates at 810 nm and 1550 nm, specifically designed to link satellite-to-ground and fiber-based quantum networks. By using a Sagnac interferometer with a PPLN crystal, they achieved high-quality entanglement in both polarization and energy-time bases. This provides a scalable and robust component for future global quantum communication infrastructures.
main_problem: The need for a quantum interconnect that bridges the gap between free-space (satellite) and fiber-based (terrestrial) quantum communication networks by providing a dual-wavelength entanglement source.
main_result: Demonstration of a robust, phase-stable source providing simultaneous polarization and energy-time entanglement at 810 nm and 1550 nm with high visibility (up to 99.5%) and high fiber coupling efficiency.
method: Spontaneous Parametric Down-Conversion (SPDC) in a periodically poled lithium niobate (PPLN) crystal placed within a polarization Sagnac interferometer, followed by Franson-type interference and quantum state tomography.
model_or_system: A bulk-scale experimental platform using a 532 nm pump laser, a 10 mm-long MgO-doped PPLN crystal, and a Sagnac interferometer configuration to generate non-degenerate photon pairs.
key_observables: Spectral brightness, entanglement visibility (polarization and energy-time), fiber coupling efficiency, heralding efficiency, and Coincidence-to-Accidental Ratio (CAR).
important_parameters: Wavelengths at 810 nm and 1550 nm; spectral brightness of 4800 pairs/s/mW/GHz; pump power up to 125 mW; entanglement fidelities near 99%.
main_assumptions: Assumes a hybrid link loss of approximately 30 dB (15 dB free-space and 15 dB fiber) for the predicted secret key rate calculations.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the experimental setup; Figure 2 illustrates the evolution of CAR and coincidence rates with pump power; Figure 3 displays energy-time interference fringes; Figure 4 presents polarization visibility and the reconstructed density matrix.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the motivation for hybrid networks, describes the experimental Sagnac-based SPDC setup, details the characterization of entanglement degrees of freedom, provides a comparative analysis with other sources, and concludes with performance predictions for a hybrid QKD link.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant to quantum optics and quantum networks as it demonstrates a practical hardware solution for interfacing different quantum communication architectures using hyperentanglement.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Schematic of the highly non-degenerate entangled photon pair source. The polarization dependent Sagnac con- figuration enables to exploit both energy-time and polariza- tion entanglement by exploiting the two counter propagating modes within the Sagnac loop. The periscope inside the in- terferometer (PS) applies a perfect 90◦polarization rotation on the three interacting wavelengths. The Glan-Thompson polarizer (GT) also acts as an achromatic polarizing beam- splitter defining the two counter-propagating modes focused into the periodically poled lithium niobate (PPLN). Further chromatic elements are used on individual modes such as two dichroic mirrors with cutoff respectively at...

FIG. 2. Evolution of the CAR and the coincidence rate in regard to the pump power. The CAR is fitted with the inverse of the pump power. This measurement is done exploiting the full range of optical power provided by our pump laser.

FIG. 3. Energy-time entanglement fringes for the Franson- type experiment. The phase shift is expressed in Volt as we linearly controlled a piezo-electric stack mounted mirror. The measurement has been performed for a pump power of 35 mW
FIG. 5. Hybrid QKD link with a 2.5km free space link be- tween a reflective collimator and the Observatoire de la Cˆote d’azur telescope.

FIG. 4. top : Raw polarization visibility of the bulk Sagnac source, in the horizontal (H), vertical (V), diagonal (D) and antidiagonal (A) linear polarization. The cos2 function used to fit the experimental data report a net visibility of 99.5% ± 0.1% for the H/V basis and 99.3% ± 0.1% for D/A basis. Bottom : Graphical representation of the real (left) and imaginary (right) parts of the density matrix extracted from experimental tomography measurements. The calculated pu- rity is 98, 1% while the fidelity to the Bell state | Φ+⟩reaches 99%.
